text: Source of truth for brand copy: Style rules always win over vocabulary, which always wins over examples. Canonical vocabulary in the glossary takes priority over invented alternatives. Existing copy examples are structural reference only — anti-patterns are flagged and must never be imitated.

Canonical vocabulary — use these exact terms: HSA (not 'HSA account'), HRA (not 'HRA account'), FSA / HCFSA (not 'flex account'), COBRA as-is, EOB (not 'benefits statement'), Reimburse Me (product feature, not 'reimbursement request'), Pay a Bill (product feature, not 'bill payment'), Plan year (not 'benefit year'), Spend period (not 'spending window'), Payroll contributions (not 'salary contributions'), Notional accounts as-is, Forfeitures (not 'lost funds'), Excess contributions (not 'overpayments'), Excess earnings (not 'bonus earnings'), IRS limit (not 'government limit'), Plan enrollment (not 'plan registration'), Participant (not 'member' or 'beneficiary'), Benefit card (not 'debit card' or 'HSA card').

Anti-patterns — flag and correct these: 'Effortlessly upload files by simply scanning' → 'Scan the QR code to upload files.' | 'Easily track enrollment details, fund movements and related information' → 'Track enrollment details, fund movements, and related information.' | 'Great news! Additional accounts have been linked' → 'Additional accounts linked to your login.' | 'You're almost there... Finish registration' → 'Verify your email to finish registration.' | 'Don't Forget to Finish Your Account Verification' → 'Finish Your Account Verification' | 'Friendly reminder — you have an uncashed check waiting.' → 'You have an uncashed check waiting.' | 'Just a reminder that you have a $[amount] contribution scheduled' → 'A contribution of $[amount] is scheduled' | 'Congratulations on Your Benefits Enrollment!' → 'Your Benefits Enrollment Is Confirmed' | 'Your Statement is Now Available!' → 'Your Statement Is Now Available' | 'Expense Activity Update - Action Required' → 'Expense Activity Update — Action Required' (em-dash, not hyphen) | 'Unfortunately, the payment was unable to be processed' → 'The payment didn't go through' | 'This may effect your tax filing' → 'This may affect your tax filing'.

Reports section card descriptions: Prefund Invoice — 'View prefunding invoice details, including plan enrollments, policy election amounts, and funding calculations.' | Contribution Invoice — 'Reconcile contribution invoicing and view invoice details. This report is limited to contribution funded accounts.' | Replenishment Invoice — 'Replenishment invoices fund payments and reimbursements. Reconcile invoices and view details.' | Health Plan Enrollment — 'View employee health plan deductible enrollments, coverage details, and HRA status.' | Participant Account Activity — 'View participant account activity, including claims, contributions, fees, balances, and enrollment details.' | Claim Activity — 'Track participant claims, including submissions, statuses, payments, and plan year data.' | Payroll Contributions — 'View payroll contribution details for HSA and notional accounts.'

Modal copy — Request Excess Contribution Withdraw: Title: 'Request Excess Contribution Withdraw' | Description: 'Excess contributions are amounts you put into your HSA beyond the IRS annual limit, which must be corrected to avoid taxes and penalties.' | Excess amount label: 'Amount entered will be debited from your HSA.' | Warning title: 'Excess contribution correction withdrawal' | Checkbox: 'I understand the above rules' (no period, sentence style).

Reimbursement flow — Let's Find Your Receipt: Page title: 'Let's Find Your Receipt' | Body: 'Let's start by adding your documentation. This could be a receipt, bill, or an EOB. We'll be able to pull some details from the document automatically.' | Contextual link: 'Have a bill you haven't paid yet? Send your payment directly to the provider here – Pay a Bill.' | Upload area: 'Drag or browse a picture of your receipt' | QR description: 'Scan the QR code with your device's camera to upload files.' (not 'Effortlessly upload files by simply scanning…').

Plan settings — Spend limits (Healthcare FSA): Tooltip Total plan limit: 'Limits the total amount that can be spent across the plan within the designated spend period, regardless of individual participants.' | Tooltip Person spend limit: 'Limits the amount that can be spent per person within the plan during the designated spend period.' | Dropdown options: 'Total plan limit', 'IRS limit', 'Person spend limit', 'Spend category spend limit', 'Unlimited', 'Monthly', 'Plan year'.

Email copy — Account & onboarding: Benefits enrollment welcome headline: 'Your Benefits Enrollment Is Confirmed' | Body: 'Take full advantage of your benefits with [Partner Name]. Here you can access your [account names].' | Checklist items end with period: 'View your account balance and manage expenses.' 'Request payments to your provider or reimburse yourself.' 'Activate and manage your benefit card.' | Button: 'Register' | Account verification reminder headline: 'Finish Your Account Verification' | Complete registration headline: 'Complete Your Registration' | Additional accounts linked headline: 'Additional Accounts Added to Your Login'.

Email copy — Security & access: Password reset headline: 'Reset Your Password' | Warning block: 'Do not share this email with anyone. Customer service representatives will never ask you for this information.' | Contact info updated: 'Your Contact Information Has Been Updated' | Body pattern: 'Your {field} has been updated to {new value}. If you didn't make this change, contact us immediately.'

Email copy — Contributions & investments: Contribution processed: 'Your Contribution Has Processed' | Body: 'Your contribution of $[amount] processed on [date]. Funds will be available to spend or invest within [hold days] days.' | Upcoming contribution: 'Upcoming Contribution to Your Account' | Problem: 'Problem Processing Your Contribution' | Body: 'the payment didn't go through. To reapply it, create another contribution request with updated banking information.' | Investment purchase: 'Investment Purchase Initiated'.

Email copy — Claims & payments: Expense activity action required: 'Expense Activity Update — Action Required' (em-dash) | No action required: 'Expense Activity Update — No Action Required' | Payment problem: 'Action Required: Problem Processing Your Payment' | Payment method updated: 'Payment Method Updated' | Body: 'Your changes are saved for future Reimburse Me requests.' | Check waiting: 'Your Check Is Waiting' | Check expired: 'Check Expired: Money Returned to Your Account'.

Email copy — Account status & alerts: Card status: 'Card Is [Active/Locked/Closed/Out for Delivery]' | New bank account added: 'New Bank Account Added' | Statement: 'Your Statement Is Now Available' | Account deadline: 'Account Deadline Alert' | Body: 'Your plan year is coming to an end. Check your account deadlines so you don't lose any money.' | HSA exception (always separate): 'Your HSA money never expires. There are no deadlines to use your money.' | Account expires: 'Your Account Expires Soon' | Trigger milestone: 'You've Met Your [Trigger Type]'.

Email copy patterns: Headlines use Book Style Capitalization, 4–6 words (up to 8 when context requires). No exclamation marks. One CTA button per email. Book Style CTAs: 'Log In', 'Register', 'Verify', 'Reset Password'. Sign-off always: 'The {partner name} Team'. Fallback link always: 'Button above doesn't work? Try this link:'. Footer asterisk for balance dates: '* Balance as of MM/DD/YYYY'. Checklist items end with period. HSA exceptions always called out separately.

Writing patterns to follow: Verb-first in actions — View / Track / Manage / Reconcile / Submit / Review / Scan / Finish. Lists use 'including': 'including claims, contributions, and fees'. Oxford comma required. No period unless 2+ sentences. Technical terms used as-is: HSA, HRA, notional accounts, forfeitures, COBRA. Em-dash (—) for separating clauses, never hyphen (-). Active voice only. No marketing filler: powerful, seamlessly, robust, streamline, effortlessly, simply, easily, just, unlock, empower, elevate, amazing, delightful, friendly reminder, great news, don't forget. No exclamation marks. No ellipses in functional copy.

Modal copy — ER Reactivation (in-progress action): Use present progressive for actions still in progress, not past tense. Early: '[ER] has been reactivated' → Final: 'Reactivating [Employer 1]'. Body: '[Employer 1] and any selected plans are being reactivated using their previous settings. See the additional steps below to complete setup.' Numbered step headings do not get a period at the end — only the description body beneath each step does. Do not embed inline navigation links (e.g. 'File Manager', 'Admin contacts') inside step description text — keep descriptions self-contained. CTA: 'Got it!' (acceptable in modal confirmation context). Next steps: 1. Review employer and plan settings — All previously saved settings have been saved. Verify these settings and add any additional plans being offered. 2. Load updated census of all employees — Employment statuses may have been updated during the termination process and will not be automatically reverted. 3. Load enrollment — Previous enrollment will not be automatically reactivated even if the plan was reactivated. Employees must be re-enrolled into each account. 4. Add or reactivate admin contacts — Previous admin access was removed at the time of termination. 5. Review payroll calendars — If your plans support scheduled contributions, review any supported payroll calendars.

Modal copy — Sync Updates Across Organizations: Modal title must be Book Style, concise noun phrase, max 5 words. Early anti-pattern: 'Select updates to push to lower-level organizations' (too long, sentence case) → Final: 'Sync Updates Across Organizations'. Body must be user-centered, not system-centered. Early anti-pattern: 'We detected updates to overridable properties. Select which updates should be pushed to lower-level organizations.' → Final: 'You've made changes that can be applied to lower-level organizations. Select which updates you'd like to apply across all lower-level organizations.' Rule: start from the user's action ('You've made changes'), not the system's internal state ('We detected'). Conditional note: 'If no updates are selected, changes will only apply to this organization and any future lower-level organizations. No other existing organizations will be updated.' CTA can include step progress context: 'Skip Sync (2/3)' communicates where the user is in a multi-step flow.

Page copy — Claim Summary (Reimburse Myself flow): Page title uses canonical term 'Claim' — 'Claim Summary', not generic 'Expense Details'. Body: 'Review the details we pulled from your document, add any missing details, and choose your payment preferences before submitting.' Warning/inline alert pattern — lead with the user benefit or consequence before the action: 'To receive your money faster, upload a clearer photo or a different document including the missing details.' Early anti-pattern: 'A clearer photo or a different document will let us try again to find the information and fill in the fields for you automatically.' (action described from system's perspective, no benefit stated for user). Inline alert heading uses question format: 'Are the missing details on your document?' Warning body adds fallback: 'If you do not have another document, you can add the missing details below.' CTAs: 'Upload a New Document', 'Why Does It Matter?' Field labels: 'Service For', 'Service Description' (deduplicated and clarified).
